rawIndexableTextContent: A Microsoft OneDrive cloud backend for Ensembles. Overview EnsemblesOneDrive provides OneDriveCloudFileSystem, an implementation of the Ensembles CloudFileSystem protocol that stores sync data on Microsoft OneDrive. It communicates directly with the Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0 using URLSession — no third-party SDK required. OneDrive supports native path-based addressing via the colon syntax (/me/drive/root:/path/to/item:), so Ensembles paths map directly without ID resolution. Authentication The included OneDriveAuthenticator handles the full OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for the Microsoft Identity Platform, stores tokens in the Keychain, and automatically refreshes expired tokens. Azure Portal setup: Go to Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → App registrations. Click New registration. Enter a name for your app. Under Supported account types, select Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts (or a narrower scope if appropriate). Click Register and note the Application (client) ID. Go to Authentication → Add a platform → Mobile and desktop applications. Set the redirect URI (e.g., msauth.com.yourapp://auth). Ensure Access tokens and Refresh tokens are enabled under Implicit grant and hybrid flows if needed. Code:  On subsequent launches, the authenticator restores tokens from the Keychain and refreshes them automatically. The default scopes are Files.ReadWrite and offline_access (the latter is required for refresh tokens). For multi-tenant scenarios, configure the tenant parameter:  For apps that manage tokens externally, pass a static access token:  Setup  API Details The backend uses the Microsoft Graph API v1.0 with path-based addressing. All file operations use the /me/drive/root:/path: syntax for direct path resolution. Operation Graph API Endpoint Connect / Identity GET /me File existence GET /me/drive/root:/path: Create directory PATCH /me/drive/root:/path: (folder facet) List directory GET /me/drive/root:/path:/children Remove item DELETE /me/drive/items/{id} Upload PUT /me/drive/root:/path:/content Download GET /me/drive/root:/path:/content  Error Handling HTTP errors are mapped to EnsembleError cases: 401 Unauthorized maps to EnsembleError.authenticationFailure. 404 Not Found maps to EnsembleError.fileAccessFailed. Other HTTP errors map to EnsembleError.serverError. Dependencies This module has no external dependencies beyond Foundation and the core Ensembles framework. All network communication uses URLSession.
title: EnsemblesOneDrive
summary: A Microsoft OneDrive cloud backend for Ensembles.

summary: Returns a valid access token, refreshing if expired. Called by OneDriveCloudFileSystem before each API request.

rawIndexableTextContent: A cloud file system backed by the Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0 using URLSession.  Overview This backend communicates directly with Microsoft’s Graph API — no third-party SDK is required. Authentication is handled via an OAuth 2.0 access token passed as a Bearer header on every request. Microsoft Graph supports native path-based addressing using the colon syntax: /me/drive/root:/path/to/item:. This means Ensembles paths map directly to OneDrive paths without any ID resolution or caching — similar to the pCloud backend. Two initialization paths are available:  Setup Register an app in the Azure Portal under Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration Under Authentication, add a platform: Mobile and desktop applications Set the redirect URI (e.g. msauth.com.yourapp://auth) Note the Application (client) ID from the overview page Configure the authenticator and file system:  The authenticator stores tokens in the Keychain and refreshes them automatically. The default scopes are Files.ReadWrite and offline_access (required for refresh tokens). The default tenant is common (supports both personal and work accounts). Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. All network calls go through a shared URLSession instance, which is thread-safe. Mutable state (loggedIn flag) is accessed only from async methods that are serialized by the ensemble’s operation queue.
title: OneDriveCloudFileSystem
summary: A cloud file system backed by the Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0 using URLSession.

rawIndexableTextContent: Manages OAuth 2.0 authentication for Microsoft OneDrive via Microsoft Identity Platform.  Overview Handles the full OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow: opening the browser, exchanging the authorization code for tokens, storing tokens in the Keychain, and automatically refreshing expired access tokens. Interactive authorization requires AuthenticationServices and is available on iOS 16+ and macOS 13+. Token refresh and Keychain storage work on all platforms including tvOS and watchOS.  Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. Mutable state (stored credentials) is accessed only from async methods that are serialized by callers.
title: OneDriveAuthenticator
summary: Manages OAuth 2.0 authentication for Microsoft OneDrive via Microsoft Identity Platform.

rawIndexableTextContent: Fetches a token representing the current user of the cloud file system. Often this will be the user or login name.
title: fetchUserIdentity()

rawIndexableTextContent: Whether the user has previously authorized (has a stored refresh token).

summary: Performs interactive OAuth 2.0 authorization using ASWebAuthenticationSession. Opens the Microsoft sign-in page, captures the authorization code, and exchanges it for access and refresh tokens.
